sglang.title: Fix GPU OOM
sglang.author: kkHuang-amd
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
For long context length case, static memory fraction only drop 10% for mem_fraction use.
If the model context length is > 8k, it will hit OOM in ci testing without this.
Modifications
Adjust the memory fraction size in model_runner.py when context length > 8k

sglang.title: [OAI] Support non-normalized logprobs in OpenAI server
sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, the SGLang backend will normalize the logprobs returned to the user by applying top_p in top_p_normalize_probs_torch. This is not consistent with OpenAI API. In OpenAI official API, the logprobs returned to the user is independent of top_p.
This PR intends to fix the gap.

Modi

sglang.title: Support Phi-4 Multi-Modal (text + vision only)
sglang.author: lifuhuang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Support Phi4-MM model with text + vision.
Modifications
This change introduced the basic text + image support.

It's worth noting that the current MMMU run (without LoRA) is lower than advertised because Phi4MM relies on LoRA for full image understanding capabilities. However, LoRA suppor

sglang.title: Sgl-router Prometheus metrics endpoint and usage track metrics
sglang.author: upfixer
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Adding a Prometheus metric endpoint exposes key usage statistics of sgl-router. With these metrics, operators can easily integrate with Prometheus/Grafana to monitor request rates, latencies, error counts, and other relevant information.

vllm.title: [BugFix][Spec Decode] Improve Prefix Caching Logic in Speculative Decoding
vllm.author: WoosukKwon
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Currently, prefix caching is inaccurate (too conservative) during speculative decoding.
This PR updates the logic to use num_draft_tokens (the actual number of scheduled draft tokens) instead of len(spec_token_ids), which may include unscheduled draft tokens.

Starting with a fix similar to #15009. We hit this when instantiating an AsyncLLM with DeepSeek models which have custom configs.
I think this was meant to be handled by calling maybe_register_config_serialize_by_value() in  EngineCoreProc's run_engine_core(), but it seems to be missed, at least in

vllm.title: [BUGFIX] catch subclass first for try...except
vllm.author: andyxning
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Python will match first exception when multi except exists. Since LocalEntryNotFoundError is a subclass of EntryNotFoundError, except LocalEntryNotFoundError should be the first except clause otherwise except LocalEntryNotFoundError will not be called.
